summary:
The agent reasons that while there’s no explicit study cited, existing knowledge links short stature—measured as a form of linear growth—to both prenatal factors and broader developmental patterns affecting all bodily measures including circumferential ones. Thus, based on shared underlying physiological mechanisms during human development, it concludes that adult body circumferences are reasonably expected to be correlated with height due to their common origin in early growth trajectories. 

Final Answer: $\boxed{\text{Yes}}$
